questions.question: Identify the elementary row operation performed on matrix A to produce matrix B. A = [[1, 3], [2, 4]], B = [[1, 3], [0, -2]]
expectedPoints: This is a Type III operation (adding a multiple of one row to another).
It is used to create a zero in the first column of the second row.

questions.question: Using elementary row operations, find the inverse of the matrix A = [[2, 5], [1, 3]]. Show all steps.
expectedPoints: Set up the augmented matrix [A | I]: [[2, 5, |, 1, 0], [1, 3, |, 0, 1]].
Perform operations to turn A into I. Step 1: R₁ → (1/2)R₁ to get a leading 1: [[1, 2.5, |, 0.5, 0], [1, 3, |, 0, 1]].
Step 2: R₂ → R₂ - R₁ to eliminate the first element in R₂: [[1, 2.5, |, 0.5, 0], [0, 0.5, |, -0.5, 1]].
Step 3: R₂ → 2R₂ to get a leading 1 in R₂: [[1, 2.5, |, 0.5, 0], [0, 1, |, -1, 2]].
Step 4: R₁ → R₁ - 2.5R₂ to eliminate the second element in R₁: [[1, 0, |, 3, -5], [0, 1, |, -1, 2]].
